steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says her publications are frequently cited, then clarifies after a dash: "other economists clearly find her studies valuable." The blank must describe what the frequent citations do to the usefulness of her research.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — diminishes: means to reduce in importance — the opposite of what frequent citations suggest. ✗
  • Choice B — underscores: means to emphasize or highlight. Frequent citations emphasize how useful her work is, matching the passage's confirmation that economists value her studies. ✓
  • Choice C — prevents: implies blocking an outcome — no sense that citations block anything here. ✗
  • Choice D — misrepresents: implies distortion or falsehood — contradicts the passage's positive framing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
